note: Signal and wave propagation speeds in various media. Values are representative engineering figures; exact values depend on material grade, wavelength, temperature, and pressure. Always verify against manufacturer datasheets or primary sources for precision applications.

in_vacuum.notes: Defined exactly since 1983 (SI); used to define the metre. Symbol: c

in_air_stp.notes: At standard temperature and pressure (STP, 0 °C, 1 atm). Varies slightly with humidity.

in_water.notes: Pure water at 20 °C, 589 nm (sodium D line)

in_glass_generic.notes: Varies significantly by glass type and wavelength

in_diamond.notes: Highest refractive index of common natural materials

fiber_optic.description: Optical fiber propagates light at roughly 2/3 of c due to the refractive index of silica glass (~1.47–1.5 at 1550 nm).

single_mode_os2.notes: ITU-T G.652D standard single-mode fiber (OS2). Most common for long-haul and datacenter interconnects.

single_mode_os1.notes: ITU-T G.652 at 1310 nm window. Slightly higher loss than 1550 nm window.

multimode_om3_om4.notes: 50/125 µm multimode fiber (OM3/OM4). Used for short-reach datacenter links up to ~400 m.

latency_examples.notes: ~5 ms additional latency from routing, amplifiers, switching

copper_cable.description: Electrical signals in copper travel at a fraction of c determined by the velocity factor (VF) of the dielectric.

coaxial_rg58.notes: RG-58A/U coaxial cable; common in 10BASE2 Ethernet and RF

coaxial_rg6.notes: RG-6; cable TV, satellite dish cabling

pcb_trace_fr4.notes: FR-4 PCB substrate; er (dielectric constant) ≈ 4.0–4.5. Critical for high-speed signal integrity.

free_space.notes: Radio waves travel at c in free space. Atmospheric effects are negligible for most applications.

gps_one_way_delay_ms.notes: GPS satellite to receiver. Ionospheric delay adds 1–10 ns (L1 band).

wifi_indoor_propagation.notes: RF propagation delay is negligible for indoor distances (<30 m ≈ 100 ns). Latency is dominated by protocol overhead.

sound.description: Acoustic wave propagation speeds for comparison.

in_air_20c.notes: At 20 °C, 50% RH, sea level. Temperature coefficient: +0.6 m/s per °C.

in_steel.notes: Longitudinal wave in steel (E ≈ 200 GPa, ρ ≈ 7850 kg/m³)

in_concrete.notes: Approximate; varies widely with aggregate and moisture

memory_bandwidth_latency_reference.note: For context: typical round-trip latency from CPU to RAM (DDR4) is 50–70 ns; NVMe SSD ~20–100 µs; SATA SSD ~50–100 µs; HDD ~5–10 ms.
memory_bandwidth_latency_reference.ddr4_latency_ns: 50–70 (unloaded; DRAM CAS latency + memory controller overhead)
memory_bandwidth_latency_reference.ddr5_latency_ns: 60–80 (slightly higher absolute latency than DDR4 at comparable frequencies)
